dialogue_text:
User: [Original_Sentence]: hey!! saw this crazy thing happen at the park the other day when an old dude totally photobombed my birthday pic. (professionalism.)
User: User: I want to change how I start this. The greeting feels too informal and 'totally photobombed' seems unprofessional. Can you help make it more suitable?
Assistant: Assistant: How about this: 'I would like to share an intriguing experience that I encountered at the park the other day when an older gentleman inadvertently appeared in my birthday photograph.'
User: User: That sounds more professional! Let's use that.
User: [Original_Sentence]: Mom was acting all weird, telling me to just ignore it?? 🙄 (subjective.)
User: User: Next, I feel 'acting all weird' isn’t very objective. What can we do here?
Assistant: Assistant: You could say, 'Mom displayed a rather sad expression—seriously, what’s up with that?'
User: User: That works better! Let’s go with that.
User: [Original_Sentence]: It was kinda funny but then she got this really sad look—seriously, what's up with that? (language.)
User: User: Should we rewrite the part about it being 'kinda funny'? I want it to remain respectful but also capture the moment.
Assistant: Assistant: How about, 'It was rather amusing, yet I noticed she displayed a rather sad expression—seriously, what’s up with that?'
User: User: Perfect! That's more aligned with my intention.
User: [Original_Sentence]: Anyway, fast forward a bit and I see this other random taking pics around our house. (transitions.)
User: User: I want this part to be more structured. It feels a bit abrupt.
Assistant: Assistant: You could modify it to 'Anyway, fast forward a bit and I saw another individual taking pictures around our house.'
User: User: That’s much cleaner! Let's implement that.
User: [Original_Sentence]: When I told mom, she just looked really worried. Odd, right? (phrasing.)
User: User: How can we maintain professionalism in this sentence? 'Looked really worried' also feels too casual.
Assistant: Assistant: You might say, 'When I informed mom, she just looked very concerned. Odd, right?'
User: User: That’s a good change. Let's proceed with it.
User: [Original_Sentence]: BTW, as time went by the camera-happy people kept showing up, like some kind of weird pattern. (informal.)
User: User: We need to refine the phrase 'camera-happy people' and clarify 'weird pattern'.
Assistant: Assistant: You could say, 'As time progressed, the camera-happy individuals continued to appear, resembling an unusual pattern.'
User: User: Yes, that's much clearer and more formal. Let’s use that.
User: [Original_Sentence]: I kept trying to figure it out but no one wanted to talk about it. UGH, adults are SO annoying. (expressions.)
User: User: This part feels too casual compared to the rest. Any ideas?
Assistant: Assistant: What if we say, 'I kept attempting to understand the situation, but no one wished to discuss it. UGH, adults are SO annoying.'? We can keep the last part as is, given the tone you've set.
User: User: I agree. That keeps the flow intact, so let’s go with your suggestion.
User: [Original_Sentence]: And there I was, on my 23rd bday, this woman just sits next to me. (clarity.)
User: User: Let's revise this sentence for professionalism. The phrase 'on my 23rd bday' sounds too casual.
Assistant: Assistant: A better option might be, 'And there I was, on my 23rd birthday, when this woman just sat next to me.'
User: User: That feels much more appropriate. Let’s implement this change.
User: [Original_Sentence]: Her outfit was like she jumped out of history books, lol. (comparisons.)
User: User: The 'jumped out of history books' should be more straightforward and professional.
Assistant: Assistant: You could say, 'Her attire was distinctly reminiscent of past decades, which caught my attention.'
User: User: Yes! That works perfectly. We'll use that.
User: [Original_Sentence]: I asked mom for the tea, and she hit me with this wild time-travel story! 😲 (story'.)
User: User: This sentence doesn't feel suitable at all. What can we do here?
Assistant: Assistant: Consider changing it to, 'I inquired with mom for more details, and she shared an astonishing time-travel narrative!'
User: User: That’s a significant improvement! Let’s adopt this change.
User: [Original_Sentence]: She said it was some big day, something about today being the day I kick the bucket?? 😧 (bucket'.)
User: User: The expression 'kick the bucket' is too informal for this context.
Assistant: Assistant: You might revise it to, 'She mentioned it was a significant day, with something about today marking my potential demise??' This remains serious without being too casual.
User: User: That's a much better choice! Let’s go with that.
User: [Original_Sentence]: Apparently lots of people were coming back in time just to watch me meet my maker. WTFFFF. (maker'.)
User: User: I think 'meet my maker' should be replaced with something less casual.
Assistant: Assistant: Perhaps saying, 'According to her, numerous individuals had traveled back in time solely to witness my departure.' would be an effective alternative.
User: User: Great suggestion! We'll go with that for sure.
User: [Original_Sentence]: Anyway, see you in the next life, I gueeeessssss 🤷‍♂️! (writing.)
User: User: This closing feels overly casual and the emoji is out of place. Can we make it more formal?
Assistant: Assistant: You could end with something like, 'Anyway, see you in the next life, I suppose.'
User: User: That’s much more appropriate! Thank you for your help!

candidates.context: revised multiple informal phrasings to more formal alternatives throughout session

candidates.value: professionalize personal narrative about a time-travel birthday incident
candidates.context: explicitly asked for help to make story more suitable and professional
telling a story about people time-traveling to witness their death
spends time crafting a story and cares about language

candidates.value: avoids informal expressions and emojis in writing
candidates.context: rejected emoji in closing and preferred formal alternatives
